findings.evidence: grep shows Provenance/credit lines in Degree.lean:24-26, SquareClass.lean:39-40, Galois.lean:32, GaloisGroup.lean:31, all citing kim-em/erdos-unit-distance; CMField.lean header (lines 1-44) has none. Degree.lean:24 sets the precedent: 'migrated and generalised from the declaration sqrtTower_finrank'.
findings.fix: Add a `## Provenance` section naming the specific erdos-unit-distance declaration for the CM-field degree (in `ErdosUnitDistance/MultiquadraticField.lean`) that this construction parallels, matching the convention in Degree.lean (`sqrtTower_finrank`). If the negative-radicand argument is genuinely original rather than migrated/generalised from that source, state that explicitly.
findings.issue: No `## Provenance` section, unlike every sibling Multiquadratic file. The file derives the degree of the Erdős CM field ℚ(i,√q₀,…,√q_{g-1}) = 2^{g+1}, which is the namesake result of kim-em/erdos-unit-distance; the negative-radicand / i-adjunction step that this file isolates as `not_isSquare_prod_optionNeg` is the construction that source's CM-field degree follows. The description credits the underlying Layer-0 machinery but names no source declaration for the CM-field result itself.

summary: The new CMField.lean proves the central CM-field degree result of kim-em/erdos-unit-distance (the field ℚ(i,√q₀,…) of degree 2^{g+1}) but is the only file in the Multiquadratic directory lacking a Provenance note; its sibling files (Degree, SquareClass, Galois, GaloisGroup) all name the specific source declaration they follow. Add a provenance note naming the erdos-unit-distance CM-field declaration this construction parallels, or confirm the negative-radicand argument is original.
